paper_title: Production of biocrude via the hydrothermal liquefaction of woody biomass and upgrading into sustainable transportation fuels: A comprehensive review
Provenance: Review Table 2 [34]; Energies (2022) poplar HTL with Fe–Co/Al2O3; methods text pasted.
Ref: Awadakkam, S., Chaudhary, V., Borugadda, V. B., & Dalai, A. K. (2025). Production of biocrude via the hydrothermal liquefaction of woody biomass and upgrading into sustainable transportation fuels: A comprehensive review. *Fuel*, 395, 135062. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.fuel.2025.135062
reactor: 1.5 L stirred batch reactor, 200 rpm (salt bath)
atmosphere: N2 purge (air removed); autogenic pressure during run
pressure_reaction_MPa: Reported range 3–10 MPa (review line lists 4 MPa); N2 purge; autogenic thereafter
separation_method: Filter to solid/liquid; evaporate liquid at 60 °C → light oil (LO); wash solids twice with DCM and evaporate at 35 °C → heavy oil (HO)

paper_title: Production of biocrude via the hydrothermal liquefaction of woody biomass and upgrading into sustainable transportation fuels: A comprehensive review
Provenance: Review Table 2 [44]; reactor + separation + feed elemental pasted.
Ref: Awadakkam, S., Chaudhary, V., Borugadda, V. B., & Dalai, A. K. (2025). Production of biocrude via the hydrothermal liquefaction of woody biomass and upgrading into sustainable transportation fuels: A comprehensive review. *Fuel*, 395, 135062. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.fuel.2025.135062
pressure_reaction_MPa: N2 purge ×3; autogenic; 1 L Parr, 100 rpm; heat ≈4 °C/min
separation_method: Filtration → EA (200 mL) extraction (gives LO2) + water evaporation (LO1); residue Soxhlet with acetone (200 mL) → HO; SR dried 105 °C (12 h)
Lignin_method: Literature: Advancing Hydrothermal Liquefaction of Canadian Forestry Biomass for Sustainable Biocrude Production: Co-Solvent Integration, Co-Liquefaction, and Process Optimization; Sustainable Energy & Fuels (2025); DOI: 10.1039/D5SE00123K

paper_title: Conversion of poplar into bio-oil via subcritical hydrothermal liquefaction: Structure and antioxidant capacity
Ref: Wu X.-F.; Zhou Q.; Li M.-F.; Li S.-X.; Bian J.; Peng F. Conversion of poplar into bio-oil via subcritical hydrothermal liquefaction: Structure and antioxidant capacity. Bioresource Technology 270 (2018) 216–222.
reactor: 1 L stainless-steel Parr reactor with mechanical stirrer (≈100 rpm)
atmosphere: N2 purge (no external gas added during reaction)
separation_method: Liquid filtered → ethyl acetate extraction: water phase → evaporate to LO1; EtOAc phase → evaporate to LO2. Filter cake Soxhlet-extracted with acetone → HO; insoluble dried at 105 °C → SR.

paper_title: Experimental study on the direct liquefaction of Cunninghamia lanceolata in water
Provenance: Feed structural (lignin/α-cellulose/pentosan/extractives), ultimate (C/H/O/N), and HHV from Table 1 (db); operating window and separation from Methods (§2); per-run yields from Table 2 (Heavy oil, OD, Residue → mapped to B_Y/A_Y/C_Y); product elemental (C/H/O/N) and HHV for Heavy oil, OD, Residue at 8g/100 mL, 10 min from Table 3 (oil & residue ingested to product columns; OD composition kept in extra).
Ref: Qu Y.; Wei X.; Zhong C., Energy 28 (2003) 597–606.
reactor: Stainless steel autoclave, 250 mL, magnetic stirrer; external electric furnace; ±3 K control
separation_method: After venting gas, contents poured out; aqueous phase filtered to separate water-insoluble fraction. Filtrate evaporated at 75 °C (atm) → residue defined as 'Organics Dissolved' (OD). Water-insoluble fraction and reactor walls washed with acetone ×3; acetone solution dried at 35 °C and 0.006 MPa → 'Heavy oil'. Acetone-insoluble solids dried at 105 °C → 'Residue' (char).

paper_title: Experimental study on the direct liquefaction of Cunninghamia lanceolata in water
Provenance: Feed structural (lignin/α-cellulose/pentosan/extractives), ultimate (C/H/O/N), and HHV from Table 1 (db); operating window and separation from Methods (§2); per-run yields from Table 2 (Heavy oil, OD, Residue → mapped to B_Y/A_Y/C_Y); product elemental (C/H/O/N) and HHV for Heavy oil, OD, Residue at 8g/100 mL, 10 min from Table 3 (oil & residue ingested to product columns; OD composition kept in extra).
Ref: Qu Y.; Wei X.; Zhong C., Energy 28 (2003) 597–606.
reactor: Stainless steel autoclave, 250 mL, magnetic stirrer; external electric furnace; ±3 K control
separation_method: After venting gas, contents poured out; aqueous phase filtered to separate water-insoluble fraction. Filtrate evaporated at 75 °C (atm) → residue defined as 'Organics Dissolved' (OD). Water-insoluble fraction and reactor walls washed with acetone ×3; acetone solution dried at 35 °C and 0.006 MPa → 'Heavy oil'. Acetone-insoluble solids dried at 105 °C → 'Residue' (char).

paper_title: Synergies during hydrothermal liquefaction of cow manure and wheat straw
Provenance: Table S1 (SM) — experimental design matrix + yields via DCM extraction
Ref: dos Passos et al. 2022 — J. Environ. Chem. Eng. 10(5):108181
pressure_reaction_MPa: autogenic (~20–25 MPa @ 300–365 °C; 1 g + 8 g water; no gas feed)

paper_title: Experimental study on the direct liquefaction of Cunninghamia lanceolata in water
Provenance: Feed structural (lignin/α-cellulose/pentosan/extractives), ultimate (C/H/O/N), and HHV from Table 1 (db); operating window and separation from Methods (§2); per-run yields from Table 2 (Heavy oil, OD, Residue → mapped to B_Y/A_Y/C_Y); product elemental (C/H/O/N) and HHV for Heavy oil, OD, Residue at 8g/100 mL, 10 min from Table 3 (oil & residue ingested to product columns; OD composition kept in extra).
Ref: Qu Y.; Wei X.; Zhong C., Energy 28 (2003) 597–606.
reactor: Stainless steel autoclave, 250 mL, magnetic stirrer; external electric furnace; ±3 K control
separation_method: After venting gas, contents poured out; aqueous phase filtered to separate water-insoluble fraction. Water-insoluble fraction and reactor walls washed with acetone ×3; acetone solution dried at 35 °C and 0.006 MPa → 'Heavy oil'. Acetone-insoluble solids dried at 105 °C → 'Residue' (char).

Ref: Tian Y.; Wang F.; Djandja J.O.; Zhang S.-L.; Xu Y.-P.; Duan P.-G. Fuel 265 (2020) 116946.
reactor: Custom 1 L HP batch reactor, electric heating sleeve (3 kW), 600 rpm stirring.
atmosphere: He (internal standard), autogenous pressure during HTL
yield_basis: wt% of dry feedstock (bio-oil, solid, gas measured; aqueous by difference).

paper_title: Experimental study on the direct liquefaction of Cunninghamia lanceolata in water
Provenance: Feed structural (lignin/α-cellulose/pentosan/extractives), ultimate (C/H/O/N), and HHV from Table 1 (db); operating window and separation from Methods (§2); per-run yields from Table 2 (Heavy oil, OD, Residue → mapped to B_Y/A_Y/C_Y); product elemental (C/H/O/N) and HHV for Heavy oil, OD, Residue at 8g/100 mL, 10 min from Table 3 (oil & residue ingested to product columns; OD composition kept in extra).
Ref: Qu Y.; Wei X.; Zhong C., Energy 28 (2003) 597–606.
reactor: Stainless steel autoclave, 250 mL, magnetic stirrer; external electric furnace; ±3 K control
separation_method: After venting gas, contents poured out; aqueous phase filtered to separate water-insoluble fraction. Filtrate evaporated at 75 °C (atm) → residue defined as 'Organics Dissolved' (OD). Water-insoluble fraction and reactor walls washed with acetone ×3; acetone solution dried at 35 °C and 0.006 MPa → 'Heavy oil'. Acetone-insoluble solids dried at 105 °C → 'Residue' (char).

paper_title: Experimental study on the direct liquefaction of Cunninghamia lanceolata in water
Provenance: Feed structural (lignin/α-cellulose/pentosan/extractives), ultimate (C/H/O/N), and HHV from Table 1 (db); operating window and separation from Methods (§2); per-run yields from Table 2 (Heavy oil, OD, Residue → mapped to B_Y/A_Y/C_Y); product elemental (C/H/O/N) and HHV for Heavy oil, OD, Residue at 8g/100 mL, 10 min from Table 3 (oil & residue ingested to product columns; OD composition kept in extra).
Ref: Qu Y.; Wei X.; Zhong C., Energy 28 (2003) 597–606.
reactor: Stainless steel autoclave, 250 mL, magnetic stirrer; external electric furnace; ±3 K control
separation_method: After venting gas, contents poured out; aqueous phase filtered to separate water-insoluble fraction. Filtrate evaporated at 75 °C (atm) → residue defined as 'Organics Dissolved' (OD). Water-insoluble fraction and reactor walls washed with acetone ×3; acetone solution dried at 35 °C and 0.006 MPa → 'Heavy oil'. Acetone-insoluble solids dried at 105 °C → 'Residue' (char).

paper_title: Catalytic hydrothermal liquefaction of orange peels into biocrude: An optimization approach by central composite design
Ref: Kariim et al., J. Anal. Appl. Pyrolysis 173 (2023) 106032
reactor: Parr 4650 stainless-steel autoclave (500 mL max volume)
pressure_reaction_MPa: autogenic (sealed autoclave); ambient charge; cooled to room T
yield_basis: wt% of dry feedstock (mass % of dry feed). Biocrude = acetone/ethanol-soluble fraction after solvent removal under reduced pressure; Solid residue = acetone-insoluble; Conversion = 100 − solid residue (wt%); Gas measured by water displacement (mL/g).
separation_method: Vacuum filtration (Whatman #1); acetone wash to clear color; rotary evaporation at 79 °C under reduced pressure (~2 h) to remove solvent+water; solid residue dried at 110 °C for 6 h before weighing; gas volume measured by water displacement (mL/g at ambient).
